name: OneFlare Golden Response — Bulk Data Exfiltration (api.soledrop.co)
description: API data-exfiltration response playbook (SoleDrop lab) — golden 4-step formula. (1) ENRICH+INVESTIGATE — read src_ip straight off the alert observable (the detection's entityMappings projects src_ip onto the alert, so no query is needed for the IP), then one native SentinelOne async PowerQuery (init-query -> query-status -> DV events) for bytes pulled, endpoints enumerated, largest response. (2) THREAT INTEL — VirusTotal (primary verdict) + AbuseIPDB (secondary) native lookups on src_ip. (3) DECIDE — post an interactive Slack Block Kit message (evidence + false-positive caveat + Block/Dismiss buttons), wait for the click, branch on it. (4) ACT+WRITE BACK — APPROVED: Cloudflare IP Access Rule block -> Slack chat.update -> S1 Add Note -> verdict (True Positive Malware only if VirusTotal returned malicious, else Alert Status In Progress); DISMISSED: Slack chat.update -> S1 Add Note -> False Positive Undefined. Every destructive action is gated on the Slack approval AND (for the TP verdict) on an independent threat-intel malicious verdict. MITRE T1567 / T1030. SETUP BEFORE IMPORT: bind Slack, Cloudflare, SentinelOne (Mgmt) and SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) connections; for enrichment create free API-key connections for VirusTotal (https://www.virustotal.com/gui/join-us) and AbuseIPDB (https://www.abuseipdb.com/register). Import with a personal Console User token and publish in the same step for UI visibility.

condition.compared_value: CF-API-Exfil — Bulk Data Exfiltration via api.soledrop.co

action.description: TRIGGER: fire automatically when the CF-API-Exfil rule (api.soledrop.co) raises an alert (event alert / CREATE, matched on rule name 'CF-API-Exfil — Bulk Data Exfiltration via api.soledrop.co'). Safe to auto-run — the only destructive action (Cloudflare block) is gated behind an explicit Slack analyst approval downstream.

variables.name: pq_query
variables.value: class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' | let src_ip = src_endpoint.ip | filter src_ip = '{{singularity-response-trigger.data.observables[0].value}}' | let uri = http_request.url.url_string | let bytes = number(unmapped.EdgeResponseBytes) | let is_sensitive = uri contains:anycase('/export') OR uri contains:anycase('/download') OR uri contains:anycase('/customers') OR uri contains:anycase('/billing') OR uri contains:anycase('/models') OR uri contains:anycase('training-data') | filter uri contains:anycase('/api/v1/') | group api_requests=count(), sensitive_hits=count(is_sensitive), distinct_paths=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.path), max_bytes=max(bytes), largest_uri=max_by(uri, bytes), host=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.hostname), country=any(src_endpoint.location.country) by src_ip | sort -sensitive_hits | limit 1 | columns src_ip, api_requests, sensitive_hits, distinct_paths, max_bytes, largest_uri, host, country

action.description: STEP 1a — read the attacker identity straight off the alert. src_ip comes from the alert observable (the detection's entityMappings projects src_ip+host onto the alert), with a fallback to the first indicator. channel_id is the SOC Slack channel (placeholder C09L36A9LHF — swap for your channel). pq_query is the corroborating investigation query (counts 401/403/429 responses from src_ip and distinct UAs/hosts; the /login scoping already lives in the detection, and the query is intentionally double-quote-free so the init-query payload stays valid JSON after substitution). All three vars reference the trigger only (never each other), so a single Variable action is E1-safe. Assumption: observables[0] is src_ip (entityMappings order src_ip,host) — verify the observable index on a real alert.

STEP 1b — native SentinelOne (Mgmt) 'Create A Power Query And Get Queryid' (public_action_id 26adfd4c, POST /web/api/v2.1/dv/init-query). Launches the corroborating investigation ASYNC and returns a queryId at body.data.queryId. Uses the console DV API (ApiToken), NOT the raw SDL LRQ Bearer endpoint. Bind the SentinelOne (Mgmt) connection after import. Tune fromDate/toDate to the tenant if DELTA_NOW hour-direction differs.

STEP 1c — native SentinelOne (Mgmt) 'Get Query Status' (public_action_id 6b7c9ea5, GET /web/api/v2.1/dv/query-status) for the queryId. Single status read to keep the flow lean (no polling loop). If the query is still RUNNING for large windows, insert a Delay before this node or add one poll — do NOT add a back-edge loop.

STEP 1d — native SentinelOne (Mgmt) 'Get all Deep Visibility events from a queryId' (public_action_id 014e1093, GET /web/api/v2.1/dv/events). Returns the grouped corroborating evidence row. Downstream reads failed_logins / distinct_uas from body.data[0]. Assumption: the aggregated PQ projection surfaces as body.data[0].<column> — verify the exact JSON path against a real query result on the tenant.

STEP 2a — PRIMARY threat-intel verdict. Native VirusTotal 'Search IP address' (public_action_id 25394f56, GET /api/v3/ip_addresses/<<ip>>) on src_ip. Reads body.data.attributes.last_analysis_stats.malicious (verdict) and body.data.attributes.country (geo). Needs a free VirusTotal API-key connection (https://www.virustotal.com/gui/join-us) bound after import. continue_on_fail so a missing key degrades to notify rather than aborting.

STEP 2b — SECONDARY threat-intel. Native AbuseIPDB 'Check IP' (public_action_id 6ea08101). CAVEAT: the catalog maps this action to GET /api/v2/blacklist, which looks mislabeled — single-IP reputation is normally GET /api/v2/check with an ipAddress param (included here). VERIFY the endpoint on the bound connection; treat VirusTotal as authoritative and use AbuseIPDB (body.data.abuseConfidenceScore) only as corroboration. Needs a free AbuseIPDB API-key connection (https://www.abuseipdb.com/register).

data.name: Send Interactive Message

action.description: STEP 3a — native Slack 'Send Interactive Message' (public_action_id 43c970e2, chat.postMessage). Posts the evidence summary (src_ip, geo, VT + AbuseIPDB verdicts, failed-login count, distinct UAs) + FP caveat (shared VPN/NAT egress?) + two buttons: Block at Cloudflare (value=approved) / Dismiss (value=dismissed). Reuses the reference playbook's real Slack connection + integration IDs. Follow with wait_for_slack.

STEP 3b — pause up to 1 hour for the analyst to click Block or Dismiss, correlated to the posted message via its ts. On resume, {{wait-for-slack.body.actions[0].value}} carries the button value and {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}} the approver.

STEP 3c — branch on the analyst's click. TRUE (value != 'dismissed', i.e. 'approved') -> containment branch (Cloudflare block + write-back). FALSE ('dismissed', or a 1h timeout that leaves value empty -> also != 'approved') -> false-positive branch. No block is ever applied without an explicit approval.

STEP 4a (APPROVED) — native Cloudflare 'Create an IP Access rule' (public_action_id 9db31c31, POST /client/v4/user/firewall/access_rules/rules). Account/user-scoped block of the attacking src_ip (no zone_id needed). Reverse later with 'Delete an IP Access rule'. Bind the Cloudflare connection after import.

data.name: Slack Update Approved

action.description: STEP 4b (APPROVED) — update the original Slack message in place (chat.update) to confirm the block, naming the approver and deep-linking the alert. Reuses the reference Slack connection IDs; public_action_id is 'Post Message' with the URL overridden to chat.update.

data.name: Add Note Approved

action.description: STEP 4c (APPROVED) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Add Note to Alert' (public_action_id 34de543f). Records who approved plus the full evidence line on the alert. Reuses the reference's S1-alert connection + integration IDs. Verify the native note body schema (data.text + filter.ids) against the bound connection.

STEP 4d (APPROVED) — evidence-discipline gate on the verdict. TRUE (VirusTotal malicious > 0) -> set analyst verdict True Positive Malware. FALSE (no independent malicious verdict) -> only move status to In Progress. Per the S1 persona rules, a True-Positive verdict requires independent threat-intel confirmation, never the block action alone.

STEP 4e (APPROVED, VT malicious) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Set Analyst Verdict as True Positive Malware' (public_action_id 8ec867d8). Only reached when VirusTotal independently confirmed the IP as malicious. Terminal node of this branch.

STEP 4e (APPROVED, no TI confirmation) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Set Alert Status to In Progress' (public_action_id b9658ad8). Block was applied on analyst approval but VirusTotal did not confirm malicious, so the alert is left In Progress (max classification SUSPICIOUS — Pending Confirmation), not marked True Positive. Terminal node of this branch.

data.name: Slack Update Dismissed

action.description: STEP 4a (DISMISSED) — update the original Slack message (chat.update) to record that the analyst treated the alert as a false positive; no block applied. Reuses the reference Slack connection IDs.

data.name: Add Note Dismissed

action.description: STEP 4b (DISMISSED) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Add Note to Alert' (public_action_id 34de543f). Records the dismissal, that no block was applied, and the rationale. Reuses the reference's S1-alert connection IDs.

STEP 4c (DISMISSED) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Set Analyst Verdict as False Positive Undefined' (public_action_id fb264d94). Closes the loop by recording the analyst's FP verdict on the alert. Terminal node of the dismissed branch.
